meaning: n.发光体(如日、月等天体),知识渊博的人
examples: Rubin, the daughter of a window cleaner and housewife from New Jersey, already had experience in writing about an Italian luminary from centuries past.
The luminary was a golden-haired, beaming, mild-eyed, God-like creature, gazing down in the vigour and intentness of youth upon an earth that was brimming with interest for him.
Reinhart, 48, is the chief executive of Luminary Micro, a three-year-old semiconductor firm in Austin, Tex.
To a standard silicon layout from ARM, Luminary adds functions like Ethernet connections, flash memory and motion-control hardware.
He says the rewards of switching to Luminary Micro's architecture will only increase over time.
